pattern: Counting numbers with all-distinct digits up to a bound is a digit-DP that tallies shorter lengths combinatorially and then walks the bound's digits prefix by prefix.
hints: Split the count into numbers with fewer digits than n (easy combinatorics) plus numbers with the same digit length staying under n.
For a length L with leading digit nonzero, the count of all-distinct numbers is 9 * 9 * 8 * ... using the falling factorial of remaining slots.
Walk the digits of n left to right; at each position add the choices smaller than the current digit (and unused) times the arrangements of the remaining slots, stopping if a digit repeats.

edge_inputs.description: three digit value with repeats inside the range
exactly a number whose own digits repeat

stress.description: random n across the full range to cross-check digit-DP against the brute oracle on small values
